comment: FR-SPD-002 within-clip freeze, late sample: same clip as time-remap-freeze-hold-early (curve 0->0, 1/4->2, 3/2->2) sampled half a second later at t=1/1. The zero-slope span must still decode source frame 192 - the two fixtures ship byte-identical reference content, which is exactly the held-frame guarantee. Dropping the curve (1x) decodes frame 96 and flattening it to its constant-rate chord (4/3x) decodes frame 128 - measured failures maxDeltaE=61.3 and 42.6 against this reference. Tolerance 10 is ~2x the measured cross-machine ProRes/color-conversion noise floor (maxDeltaE 4.614 on the CI runner vs 0.000 locally) and every wrong-variant signal is >= 2x the tolerance; it is tighter than the repo's usual decode-path 25 so the suite-wide weakest FR-SPD-002 signal (22.2 on time-remap-ramp-quad) stays discriminating.
